summary: The public/external protocol surface of AFI is thin and inconsistent. The only deployable public ingest API is afi-gateway's tenant-scoped POST /api/v1/signals, which performs a 4-field presence check (signalId/epochId/market/timeframe) and writes the canonical VaultedSignalRecord straight into Mongo with NO USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 / vault-schema validation; a parallel, more rigorous ingest surface lives in afi-reactor (POST /api/webhooks/tradingview + POST /api/ingest/cpj, AJV-validated), so there is no single canonical public protocol API and the two entry points disagree on what 'valid' means. The official SDKs (afi-sdk-python, afi-sdk-ts) are empty 1.0.0-versioned scaffolds (README + manifest only) whose usage examples (`from afi_sdk import AFIClient`, `client.signals.submit(...)`) cannot run, and both, plus afi-starters, frame the surface as 'API clients for afi-reactor' rather than the canonical USS/CPJ dialect. External-validator integration is the largest gap: gateway/reactor/core all declare that 'validator certification [is] moved to external certification layer' yet NO external-validator service, endpoint, or interoperability schema exists; the only validator-facing schema (afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json) configures an internal mint daemon (Snapshot space, AFIMintCoordinator address), not a third-party validator, and the portable spec's promised cross-repo 'Replay Contract' for third-party validators is unimplemented. Net: an external builder cannot reliably submit a schema-valid signal through one canonical API nor independently certify/replay a mint without org infra.

answers.question: I1: What is the public protocol API surface (the endpoints/clients exposed for external builders to interact with the protocol)?
answers.answer: There are two disjoint HTTP ingest surfaces and no published SDK, so the 'public protocol API surface' is fragmented. (A) REFERENCE GATEWAY (afi-gateway) exposes a tenant-scoped REST API advertised at server start: GET /healthz, POST/GET /api/v1/api-keys (+ /:keyId/revoke), POST /api/v1/signals (the signal ingest), and public GET /api/v1/skills(/:id, /capabilities) (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:77,82,96,123,144; advertised afi-gateway/src/server.ts:71-76). The ingest route normalizes the body into the canonical VaultedSignalRecord (imported from afi-infra, afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:8) and persists via vault.upsert (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134). It is a deployable, Mongo-backed path (afi-gateway/src/server.ts:58 buildApp() with createVaultFactoryFromEnv -> Mongo). (B) REACTOR (afi-reactor) exposes a second, separate public ingest surface: POST /api/webhooks/tradingview and POST /api/ingest/cpj (afi-reactor/src/server.ts:159,290; advertised :442-443), plus GET /health. Normatively the portable spec names the ingest boundary's valid entry dialect as USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 / lens extensions over 'afi-gateway, webhooks, SDKs' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:55). (C) SDKs: afi-sdk-python and afi-sdk-ts are advertised as the 'official' client surface but contain no code (see I3). The gateway's only programmatic client is a dev/demo HTTP wrapper to the reactor Froggy endpoint (afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:117-138 runFroggyTrendPullback -> /api/webhooks/tradingview).
answers.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:77; afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:123; afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134; afi-gateway/src/server.ts:71-76; afi-reactor/src/server.ts:159; afi-reactor/src/server.ts:290; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:55; afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:117-138

answers.question: I2: What are the external-validator integration gaps (what does a third party need to integrate/certify/replay, and what is missing)?
answers.answer: External-validator integration is essentially unbuilt. (1) NO EXTERNAL VALIDATOR INTERFACE EXISTS: the gateway, reactor pipeline, and the scored-signal response type all state that 'Validator certification [is] moved to external certification layer' / 'NOT Reactor's responsibility' (afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:46; afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:93; afi-reactor/src/types/ReactorScoredSignalV1.ts:8), but there is no service, HTTP endpoint, queue, or published interface that this external certification layer would implement or consume — reactor returns ReactorScoredSignalV1 (scoredAt/analystScore only) with no certification hook (afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:50-80). (2) NO INTEROP SCHEMA FOR THIRD-PARTY VALIDATORS: the only validator-facing schema, afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json, configures an INTERNAL signal-validator orchestrator/daemon — decay interval, challenge window, mintApprovalThreshold, Snapshot space (afidao.eth), and the AFIMintCoordinator contract address/chainId (afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:7-13,38-46,77-99) — i.e. it presumes the org's own daemon holds EMISSIONS_ROLE and submits mints, not an independent validator. (3) REPLAY CONTRACT UNDELIVERED: the portable spec promises a cross-repo 'Replay Contract — what a third-party validator needs to reproduce a mint decision without org infra' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:150) and a non-mandatory Reference Implementation Index (:147), but no such checklist/artifact exists and (per Theme C/D, library findings) nothing on-chain anchors the score/content-hash/ruleset version needed to verify a mint from chain data alone. (4) AUTH/INTEROP FRICTION: the gateway ingest requires an org-issued API key (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:123 auth middleware), and a submitter's canonical analystId is silently overwritten with the gateway tenantId (afi-infra/src/tssd/TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient.ts:29-37), so external provenance cannot survive ingest. Net gap: a third party can neither submit through one canonical schema-validated API nor independently certify/replay a mint.

answers.question: I3: What is the SDK stub status (afi-sdk-python, afi-sdk-ts, and starter SDK templates)?
answers.answer: Both official SDKs are empty placeholder scaffolds, not functioning SDKs. afi-sdk-python contains only README.md and pyproject.toml (no afi_sdk package, no .py source, no tests); pyproject declares version '1.0.0' (afi-sdk-python/pyproject.toml:3) while the README's quick-start `from afi_sdk import AFIClient` / `client.signals.submit(...)` cannot import anything (afi-sdk-python/README.md:28-34), and Status admits it is a 'New repository created during multi-repo reorganization (2025-11-14)' (afi-sdk-python/README.md:41). afi-sdk-ts likewise contains only README.md and package.json; package.json points main at a non-existent build (afi-sdk-ts/package.json:6 './dist/index.js') and its test script is a stub (afi-sdk-ts/package.json:16 "echo 'Test script TBD'"), with the README example `client.signals.submit(...)` equally non-functional (afi-sdk-ts/README.md:34-38). Neither SDK validates payloads against USS/CPJ; both headline themselves as clients for the reference orchestrator: 'Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor' (afi-sdk-python/README.md:11) and 'Type-safe API clients for afi-reactor' (afi-sdk-ts/README.md:11). The afi-starters kit advertises 'starter-typescript-app — TypeScript app with afi-sdk-ts' and 'starter-python-app — Python app with afi-sdk-python' (afi-starters/README.md:18-19) but those starters do not exist on disk (only self-hosted-pipeline/ is present). Recon classifies both SDK repos STALE (empty placeholders).
answers.evidence: afi-sdk-python/pyproject.toml:3; afi-sdk-python/README.md:11; afi-sdk-python/README.md:28-34; afi-sdk-ts/package.json:6; afi-sdk-ts/package.json:16; afi-sdk-ts/README.md:11; afi-sdk-ts/README.md:34-38; afi-starters/README.md:18-19; afi-docs/specs/audit/recon/per-repo/afi-sdk-python.json:5

answers.question: I4: How deep is the gateway's ingest-validation (does it enforce the canonical USS/CPJ dialect before persisting)?
answers.answer: Gateway ingest validation is shallow and does NOT enforce the canonical dialect. normalizeSignalPayload only checks presence of four identity fields — `const required = ["signalId","epochId","market","timeframe"]` (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:26-30) — then shallow-copies the rest of the body into a VaultedSignalRecord, passing through `stages: payload.stages ?? {}`, `publicSurface: payload.publicSurface ?? {...}`, and `proprietaryDetail` unvalidated (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:44-51), and writes it directly to the Mongo TSSD vault via vault.upsert (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134). There is NO call to a USS v1.1 validator, CPJ v0.1 validator, or vault.schema.json check on this route. This contrasts sharply with the reactor surface, which runs AJV schema validation: validateUsignalV11 on /api/webhooks/tradingview (afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211) and validateCpjV01 + mapCpjToUssV11 + validateUsignalV11 on /api/ingest/cpj (afi-reactor/src/server.ts:314,331,356). The portable spec's decentralization intent is that the canonical dialect validate at the ingest boundary (USS v1.1 requires schema const + provenance{source,providerId,signalId}, per Theme A and afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239), so the gateway — the one deployable public ingest — is the weakest link: external callers can persist arbitrary non-canonical records as long as four identity fields are present.
answers.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:26-30; afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:44-51; afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134; afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211; afi-reactor/src/server.ts:314; afi-reactor/src/server.ts:356; afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239

answers.question: I5: How do the gateway and starters frame the protocol surface relative to the portable (normative-vs-reference) direction?
answers.answer: The SDKs, gateway, and the only deployment starter all present the REFERENCE stack (afi-reactor + Mongo) as if it were the protocol, contradicting the portable framing that these are non-binding reference implementations (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:115 'not binding on external validators'; :147 Reference Implementation Index 'explicitly non-mandatory'). The gateway's own AGENTS.md declares it a pure HTTP/WS client with 'No direct DB access' (afi-gateway/AGENTS.md security section) yet the live ingest route writes the canonical record straight to Mongo (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134) via a Mongo-only factory (afi-gateway/src/services/vaultFactory.ts:7-22, throws if no Mongo URI). The afi-starters self-hosted-pipeline hard-codes mongo:7 as the sole datastore (afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/docker-compose.yml:4-10) and its plugin template binds to concrete afi-reactor types as the integration contract (afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/plugins/custom.plugin.ts:1-2 `import type { Pipehead, PipelineState } from "afi-reactor/src/types/dag.js"`), while AGENTS.md calls the repo 'templates only, not runnable applications' (afi-starters/AGENTS.md run-locally section). So a new external builder cloning the SDKs/starters is steered toward reactor-as-the-API and Mongo-as-the-vault rather than the canonical USS/CPJ dialect + multi-engine vault schema.

findings.title: Public gateway ingest performs only a 4-field presence check, not canonical USS/CPJ/vault-schema validation, before writing to the vault
findings.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:26-30 'const required = ["signalId","epochId","market","timeframe"]; const missing = required.filter((k) => !identity[k]);' then afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:44-51 passes 'stages: payload.stages ?? {}', publicSurface/proprietaryDetail through unvalidated and afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134 'await vault.upsert(parsed.record)'
findings.recommendation: Validate inbound payloads against USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 (afi-config/schemas) at the gateway boundary before persisting, mirroring afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211,314,356; reject non-canonical records so external submissions cannot pollute the canonical vault.

findings.title: Two disjoint public ingest surfaces with inconsistent validation; no single canonical public protocol API
findings.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:123 'app.post("/api/v1/signals"...)' (presence-check only, Mongo write) vs afi-reactor/src/server.ts:159 '/api/webhooks/tradingview' + afi-reactor/src/server.ts:290 '/api/ingest/cpj' (AJV USS/CPJ validation at :211,:314,:356); the two entry points disagree on what a valid signal is
findings.recommendation: Define one canonical public ingest contract (schema + endpoint) that both the gateway and reactor honor; publish it in afi-config so external builders and SDKs target a single validated surface.

findings.title: 'Validator certification moved to external certification layer' but no external-validator service, endpoint, or interface exists
findings.evidence: afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:46 '- Validator certification (moved to external certification layer)'; afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:93 'validator-decision: Moved to external certification layer (not Reactor's responsibility)'; afi-reactor/src/types/ReactorScoredSignalV1.ts:8 'Validator certification, execution, and minting are NOT Reactor's responsibility' — grep finds no service/endpoint implementing that layer
findings.recommendation: Either implement and publish the external certification interface (input = scored signal, output = certification/decision) or update docs/comments to state certification is internal; an undefined 'external layer' blocks third-party validator integration.

findings.title: Official SDKs (afi-sdk-python, afi-sdk-ts) are empty 1.0.0-versioned scaffolds with non-functional usage examples
findings.evidence: afi-sdk-python/pyproject.toml:3 'version = "1.0.0"' with only README+pyproject (no afi_sdk package); afi-sdk-python/README.md:28 'from afi_sdk import AFIClient'; afi-sdk-ts/package.json:6 '"main": "./dist/index.js"' (no dist) and afi-sdk-ts/package.json:16 "test": "echo 'Test script TBD'"
findings.recommendation: Mark the SDK READMEs as pre-release/aspirational (drop the 1.0.0 implication) until a client targeting the canonical USS/CPJ ingest exists; otherwise external builders following the docs hit immediate import/install failures.

findings.title: SDK/starter API framing couples external builders to afi-reactor (reference impl) instead of the canonical USS/CPJ dialect
findings.evidence: afi-sdk-python/README.md:11 '- Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor'; afi-sdk-ts/README.md:11 'Type-safe API clients for afi-reactor'; afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/plugins/custom.plugin.ts:1-2 imports afi-reactor 'Pipehead, PipelineState' as the integration contract vs afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:147 (reference index 'explicitly non-mandatory')
findings.recommendation: Reframe SDKs/starters to target the canonical ingest dialect + afi-config schema validation, keeping afi-reactor as one optional backend, so the public surface stays portable across orchestrators.

findings.title: No machine-readable Replay Contract / external-validator interop schema; validatorConfig schema configures an internal mint daemon, not third parties
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:7-13 (required: decayCheckIntervalMs, challengeWindowDurationHours, mintApprovalThreshold, snapshotSpaceId) and :38-46 (Snapshot space 'afidao.eth') configure an org daemon; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:150 promises a third-party 'Replay Contract' that is not delivered
findings.recommendation: Author the Replay Contract checklist and an external-validator interop schema (inputs/outputs to reproduce a mint decision without org infra), distinct from the internal validatorConfig daemon settings.

findings.title: Gateway TenantScoped client silently overwrites canonical analystId with the auth tenantId, corrupting external provenance
findings.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:41 accepts payload identity.analystId but afi-infra/src/tssd/TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient.ts:29-37 'identity: { ...record.identity, analystId: this.tenantId }' replaces it on every upsert
findings.recommendation: Store the gateway tenantId in a separate gateway-scoped field; preserve the submitted SignalIdentity.analystId so replay/attribution provenance survives external ingest.

findings.title: afi-starters README advertises 9 starters (incl. afi-sdk-ts/afi-sdk-python app templates) that do not exist; only self-hosted-pipeline is present
findings.evidence: afi-starters/README.md:11-31 lists starter-signal-generator/starter-validator/.../starter-typescript-app/starter-python-app and 'cd afi-starters/starter-typescript-app'; only self-hosted-pipeline/ exists on disk (afi-starters/AGENTS.md run-locally: 'templates only, not runnable applications')
findings.recommendation: Trim the README catalog to what exists or mark unbuilt starters as 'planned'; especially do not advertise SDK-app starters while the SDKs themselves are empty scaffolds.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-gateway is a pure HTTP/WS client of AFI services with 'No direct DB access' and 'No AFI business logic here'.
contradictions.code_does: The live, deployable POST /api/v1/signals route writes the canonical VaultedSignalRecord directly into the Mongo TSSD vault via a Mongo-only factory.
contradictions.evidence: afi-gateway/AGENTS.md (Security: 'No direct DB access: All AFI data access happens via AFI HTTP/WS APIs') vs afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134 'const vault = vaultFactory(tenantId); await vault.upsert(parsed.record);' + afi-gateway/src/services/vaultFactory.ts:7-22

contradictions.doc_says: Code/comments state validator certification is performed by an 'external certification layer' that is not the reactor's responsibility.
contradictions.code_does: No external-validator service, endpoint, or interface is implemented anywhere; the scored-signal response exposes no certification hook, so external validators have nothing to integrate with.
contradictions.evidence: afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:93 'Moved to external certification layer'; afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:46; afi-reactor/src/types/ReactorScoredSignalV1.ts:8 vs afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:50-80 (ReactorScoredSignalV1 has no certification field)

contradictions.doc_says: afi-sdk-python and afi-sdk-ts are the 'official' SDKs offering 'API clients for afi-reactor' and 'Signal submission and validation helpers'.
contradictions.code_does: Both repos are empty scaffolds (README + manifest only) with version 1.0.0 and usage examples that cannot import or run; no client or validation code exists.

contradictions.doc_says: The portable spec frames afi-reactor and the Mongo vault as non-binding reference implementations.
contradictions.code_does: The SDK READMEs and the only self-hosted starter hard-couple external builders to afi-reactor as the API and mongo:7 as the sole datastore.
contradictions.evidence: afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:147 vs afi-sdk-ts/README.md:11 'API clients for afi-reactor'; afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/docker-compose.yml:4-10 'mongo: image: mongo:7'; afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/plugins/custom.plugin.ts:1-2

contradictions.doc_says: The canonical vault is multi-engine (mongodb/postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb per vault.schema.json) and Mongo is a 'default reference vault'.
contradictions.code_does: The gateway ingest and the self-hosted starter wire Mongo as the only persistence engine, with no engine selection.
